news.title: Waterways Leisure Tourism IPO Day 3: Issue subscribed 69%, GMP signals flat debut. Should you apply?
news.description: Waterways Leisure Tourism IPO: The IPO received a lukewarm response on its final day of bidding, with the issue subscribed only 69% overall. While retail investors showed strong interest, subscribing their portion three times, participation from institutional investors remained weak.

news.title: Manappuram Finance, Muthoot Finance, other gold financier stocks drop up to 3%. Here's why
news.description: Shares of gold financiers declined as a sharp fall in gold prices and a stronger US dollar weighed on sentiment. news.title: Crude oil prices crash 42% from April’s $126 peak. More downside coming?
news.description: Global oil markets are stabilizing after months of turmoil caused by the Strait of Hormuz closure and war damage. Crude prices have plummeted 42% from their April peak, with Brent falling below $73 a barrel, signaling a return to pre-conflict levels

news.title: India bonds jump ahead of auction as oil erases war-led gains

news.title: Diamonds are forever. Their value isn't.
news.description: Diamonds may hold emotional value, but they rarely hold their worth in the secondary market.

news.title: Andhra Pradesh: CM Naidu to inaugurate gold mining project at Jonnagiri
news.description: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister N Chandrababu Naidu will inaugurate the Jonnagiri gold mining project in Kurnool district on Wednesday, a ₹405 crore initiative by Geo Mysore and Deccan Gold Mines set to produce 400 kg of gold in the first year.

news.title: Gold prices fall Rs 2,200/10 gms; silver tanks Rs 3,300/kg as rate hike fears outweigh falling oil prices. Key levels to watch
news.description: Gold and silver prices fell sharply on MCX on Wednesday as a stronger U.S. dollar and rising expectations of Federal Reserve rate hikes dampened sentiment, outweighing support from easing oil prices and the gradual reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.
